issues.title: Missing Revenue Calculation
issues.problem: The query does not calculate revenue as per the business rule.
issues.fix: Add a SUM(amount) WHERE status = 'COMPLETED' calculation for revenue.

issues.title: Incorrect Lifetime Value Calculation
issues.problem: Lifetime value should only include completed transactions, but the sum includes all transactions.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Lack of Comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining complex logic.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent Naming
issues.problem: The alias 'c' for 'dim_customer' is clear, but 't' for 'fact_transactions' could be more descriptive.
issues.fix: Use more descriptive aliases, e.g., 'tx' for 'fact_transactions'.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query has critical correctness issues and could benefit from improved readability and additional comments.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause, leading to a potential division by zero error.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid the correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  WHERE status = 'FAILED'
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0) AS failed_count,
       COUNT(*) AS total_count,
       COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0) / COUNT(*) * 100 AS failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
LEFT JOIN failed_transactions ft ON t.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date
GROUP BY t.transaction_date, ft.failed_count
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with the correlated subquery. Additionally, readability was improved by adding comments and restructuring the query.

issues.title: Revenue calculation missing filter
issues.problem: Revenue is calculated without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as revenue

issues.title: Failure rate calculation incorrect
issues.line: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) * 100 as fail_pct
issues.problem: Failure rate calculation is incorrect because it should be based on the total transaction count, not just completed ones.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(t.transaction_id) * 100 as fail_pct

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can make the query harder to read and understand.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can be helpful for future maintainers.
issues.fix:
-- Added comments for better readability
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', t.transaction_date) as month,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as revenue,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as txn_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(t.transaction_id) * 100 as fail_pct
FROM fact_transactions t
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
GROUP BY m.merchant_name, month
ORDER BY month, revenue DESC;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Monthly merchant performance report
-- Shows each merchant's monthly revenue and transaction volume
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', t.transaction_date) as month,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as revenue,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as txn_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(t.transaction_id) * 100 as fail_pct
FROM fact_transactions t
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
GROUP BY m.merchant_name, month
ORDER BY month, revenue DESC;
review.summary: The query had correctness issues with revenue calculation and failure rate. Readability was improved by changing the join syntax and adding comments.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query sums all transaction amounts without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Potential performance issue with implicit join
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially less performant queries.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can make it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and a readability issue with lack of comments. It also uses an implicit join which can be replaced with an explicit join for better readability and potential performance benefits.
